text.title: Accessibility Automation Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Legal risks, VPAT traps, ARIA fix errors, overlay failures, and agent gotchas for continuous accessibility automation.

antipattern.description: Publishing AI-generated VPAT sections externally without human legal/compliance review.
antipattern.reason: VPAT drafts are legal documents. Any inaccurate conformance claim exposes the organization to legal risk. DRAFT watermark is mandatory on all AI-generated sections; human sign-off required before any external use.

antipattern.description: Auto-closing issues based on AI re-scan alone without human confirmation on real assistive technology.
antipattern.reason: AI scan confirms the code pattern changed, not that the fix works for actual users. A regression may exist that the scanner cannot detect. Human validation on VoiceOver, NVDA, or JAWS is required before closure.

antipattern.description: Deploying AI overlay widgets as the ADA Title II compliance strategy.
antipattern.reason: Overlays do not satisfy ADA Title II requirements. They are JavaScript patches that cannot fix semantic HTML issues, keyboard navigation, or focus management. Organizations that rely on overlays remain non-compliant.

antipattern.description: Agent-generated code fixes for complex ARIA widget patterns (combobox, tree, grid) applied without senior review.
antipattern.reason: ARIA widget patterns require precise role/state/property relationships. AI-generated fixes for these patterns are frequently incorrect and may introduce new accessibility failures.

antipattern.description: Sending large SPA axe JSON (100KB+) to LLM in a single prompt.
antipattern.reason: Large single-page apps generate axe reports that exceed LLM context limits. Chunk by page or component before sending; aggregate results after processing.

antipattern.description: Starting automation without an existing accessibility baseline.
antipattern.reason: Without a baseline, CI gates immediately block all deploys due to the full existing backlog. Establish a baseline first, then gate on regressions only.
